query: Blue Origin TeraWave satellite network announcement data speeds TBPS

{"query":"Blue Origin TeraWave satellite network announcement data speeds TBPS","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1759,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Blue Origin has officially unveiled TeraWave, an ambitious global communications network designed to deliver symmetrical data speeds of up to 6 terabits per second. Announced on January 21, 2026, the project marks Jeff Bezos's most direct challenge to the market dominance of SpaceX's Starlink.Jan 24, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Starlink","title":"Starlink - Wikipedia","content":"Globalstar – an operational low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation for satellite phone and low-speed data communications, covering most of the world's landmass.
"Starlink is connecting more than 4M people with high-speed internet across 100+ countries, territories and many other markets" (Tweet).
".@SpaceX is joining the effort to help get Canadians connected to high-speed Internet!
"SpaceX's Starlink network surpasses 400-satellite mark after successful launch"."},{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/news/blue-origin-introduces-terawave-space-based-network-for-global-connectivity","title":"Blue Origin Introduces TeraWave, a 6 Tbps Space-Based Network for Global Connectivity | Blue Origin","content":"Kent, WA – Blue Origin today announced TeraWave, a satellite communications network designed to deliver symmetrical data speeds of up to 6 Tbps anywhere on Earth.
Deployment of the TeraWave constellation will begin in Q4 2027."},{"url":"https://www.geekwire.com/2026/blue-origin-terawave-satellite-network/","title":"Blue Origin plans ultra-high-speed TeraWave satellite network – GeekWire","content":"Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin space venture says it’ll be ramping up an ultra-high-speed satellite data network called TeraWave, which will compete with SpaceX’s Starlink network for business from data centers, large-scale enterprises and government customers.
In contrast, TeraWave is targeting higher-end data applications with symmetrical data speeds of up to 6 terabits per second (Tbps), a rate that’s 6,000 times faster.
In today’s announcement, Blue Origin said TeraWave’s constellation would consist of 5,408 laser-linked satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO) and medium Earth orbit (MEO).
Tech consultant Tim Farrar, the founder of TMF Associates, said the emergence of TeraWave raises questions about the relationship between the two best-known companies founded by Bezos.
Or an alternative source of launch demand [for Blue Origin] in case Amazon decides to scale back their near-term space investments while they try to prove the case for Amazon Leo Gen1, before spending more money to launch a Gen2 system?”
Farrar speculated that the TeraWave initiative might represent an effort by Bezos to pressure Amazon’s current leadership to keep investing in space, or to spin off the Leo system to Blue Origin."},{"url":"https://broadbandbreakfast.com/bezoss-blue-origin-planning-satellite-broadband-service-for-enterprise-customers/","title":"Bezos’s Blue Origin Planning Satellite Broadband Service for Enterprise Customers","content":"22, 2026 – Jeff Bezos’s rocket company Blue Origin is planning a high-speed satellite broadband product for business and government customers, the company announced Wednesday.
The company is aiming to deploy the 5,408-satellite constellation, called TeraWave, beginning in the fourth quarter of 2027 and ultimately provide symmetrical speeds of up to 6 terabits per second.
The constellation would consist mostly of low-Earth orbit satellites, 5,280 of them, with the ultra-fast 6 Tbps speeds supported by 128 medium-Earth orbit units via “optical links.”
Amazon, which Bezos founded and where he chairs the board of directors, is also working to enter the space, with 180 satellites in orbit and a planned total of more than 3,200."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Blue Origin TeraWave satellite network announcement data speeds TBPS","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/news/blue-origin-introduces-terawave-space-based-network-for-global-connectivity","title":"Blue Origin Introduces TeraWave, a 6 Tbps Space-Based Network for Global Connectivity | Blue Origin","content":"Blue Origin: For The Benefit of Earth. # Blue Origin Introduces TeraWave, a 6 Tbps Space-Based Network for Global Connectivity. **Kent, WA** – Blue Origin today announced TeraWave, a satellite communications network designed to deliver symmetrical data speeds of up to 6 Tbps anywhere on Earth. This network will service tens of thousands of enterprise, data center, and government users who require reliable connectivity for critical operations. The TeraWave architecture consists of 5,408 optically interconnected satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO) and medium Earth orbit (MEO). TeraWave addresses the unmet needs of customers who are seeking higher throughput, symmetrical upload/download speeds, more redundancy, and rapid scalability. Globally distributed customers can each access speeds of up to 144 Gbps delivered using Q/V-band links from a constellation of 5,280 LEO satellites, while up to 6 Tbps can be accessed via optical links from 128 MEO satellites. Blue Origin announced the launch date for New Glenn's third mission, slated to launch no earlier than Sunday, April 19, 2026.","score":0.9328032,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/21/blue-origins-satellite-internet-network-terawave-will-move-data-at-6tbps","title":"Blue Origin's satellite internet network TeraWave will move data at 6 Tbps | TechCrunch","content":"TeraWave, Blue Origin's new satellite internet network. # Blue Origin’s satellite internet network TeraWave will move data at 6 Tbps. Jeff Bezos’ space company Blue Origin has just announced a satellite internet network called TeraWave, which will be capable of offering data speeds up to 6 Tbps, and geared toward enterprise, data center, and government customers. The TeraWave constellation will use a mix of 5,280 satellites in low-Earth orbit and 128 in medium-Earth orbit, and Blue Origin plans to deploy the first ones in late 2027. The low-Earth orbit satellites Blue Origin is building will use RF connectivity and have a max data transfer speed of 144 Gbps, while the medium-Earth variety will use an optical link that can achieve the much higher 6 Tbps speed. The announcement of the TeraWave network comes just a few months after Bezos’ other company, Amazon, announced a rebrand of its own satellite network geared toward consumers.","score":0.93086797,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.geekwire.com/2026/blue-origin-terawave-satellite-network","title":"Blue Origin plans ultra-high-speed TeraWave satellite network – GeekWire","content":"# Blue Origin unveils TeraWave, a global satellite network designed to handle terabits of data center traffic. Please review our privacy policy(opens in a new tab) for more information on how we process your data.","score":0.9088853,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.rcrwireless.com/20260123/network-infrastructure/blue-origin-steps-into-the-spotlight-with-a-6-tbps-space-network","title":"​​Blue Origin steps into the spotlight with a 6 Tbps space network","content":"## TeraWave is aimed to meet a growing demand for space-based communication in enterprise, data centers, and governments. **6 Tbps, anywhere on Earth:** Jeff Bezos’ space venture, Blue Origin, just announced TeraWave, a multi-orbit network that is designed to deliver up to 6 Tbps symmetrical data speeds anywhere on Earth, particularly in dead zones. **The bigger constellation math:** The company is looking to put roughly 5,500 satellites in orbit starting end of 2027, but the math does not add up for some. In a move that took many by surprise, Jeff Bezos’ space venture, Blue Origin, dropped a new satellite internet service on Wednesday. TeraWave’s new page on the Blue Origin website reads, “TeraWave adds a space-based layer to your existing network infrastructure, providing connectivity to locations unreachable by traditional methods,” which sounds like standard satellite internet fare — except, TeraWave promises to offer ambitious speeds of up to 6 Tbps anywhere on Earth, “particularly in remote, rural, and suburban areas where diverse fiber paths are costly, technically infeasible, or slow to deploy.”.","score":0.8872928,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.74,"request_id":"7e375ba2-4c33-42e8-b716-0ca3bd58b65d"}
